title: My Home Is Enough Today
subtitle: An affirmation for living in the home you have while building toward the one you want
excerpt: An affirmation for holding two things at once: satisfaction with the home you have today, and the legitimate goal of something better. For use when the gap between the two is running the day.

practice.whenToUse: Use on days when dissatisfaction with the current home is running the mood, or when the wanting of something better is making it hard to be in what you have.

content.text: An affirmation for two things being true at the same time: the home you have is enough for today, and the goal of something better is also real and legitimate. The two do not cancel each other out. The affirmation holds both without requiring a choice between them.

The habit of living in a home as a waiting room for the next one suspends the experience of being at home anywhere. This affirmation pulls the experience back to today. It does not require you to give up the goal. It only requires you to be in the present home while you work toward the future one.
My home is enough today. I am building toward more.
Say it when you walk in the door, or first thing in the morning while you are still in the house. Say both sentences. The first one lands the present. The second one keeps the future open.
